description_long: This house features a steeply pitched gable roof with wooden shingles and two prominent chimneys at either end. The exterior walls are constructed from a combination of rough stone on the lower level and white plaster with exposed timber framing on the upper level, evoking a traditional Tudor or medieval European style. The front facade includes a covered porch with a central entryway, flanked by three dormer windows that project from the roof. The windows are arranged in a symmetrical row on the upper story, while the lower story is mostly stone with minimal fenestration. The side elevations reveal the depth of the roof and the timber framing details, as well as the overhanging eaves. The overall structure suggests a two-story residential building designed for a historical or fantasy urban setting.
description: A two-story Tudor-style house with a steep gable roof, stone and timber exterior, and a covered front porch with dormer windows.
description_view_0: Side view showing the steep gable roof, exposed timber framing, and stone lower wall.
description_view_1: Rear view highlighting the full width of the shingled roof and symmetrical upper windows.
description_view_2: Opposite side view with similar gable and timber details, plus visible porch structure.
description_view_3: Front view featuring the covered porch, central entryway, and three dormer windows above.
attribute_entryway_design: Central wooden door with a covered porch, steps, and overhanging roof supported by timber posts.
